paragraphs.body: It's a cold and dark December night in the city of Philadelphia and we are driving back home from walking around the Christmas Village in Philadelphia where we spent the whole day shopping. We spent the day in Philly and started the day by going to Macy’s to watch the light show in the store and to do the Charle’s Dickens Village which is a family tradition to go every year at Christmas time. We spent the rest of the day looking at all the unique little trinkets that each vendor had and was selling. Look at all the amazing artwork that was being showcased like hand-painted portraits of Eagles like Jason Kelce, Nick Foles, and Brandon Grahmn and Phillies players like Bryce Harper and Mike Schmidt. Artwork made up of words that outlined famous musicians spanning from 1950s musicians to today's musicians. I ran to this one booth that had one of these beautifully done artworks that resembled The Beatles' Abbey Road and stared at the artwork for some time before my dad told me that we were going to get some churros from a truck on the other side. The food as well was delicious and was well worth waiting in the lines to get the unique sweet treats that the different vendors had. We ended our day at our favorite little restaurant in the city called Smokin’ Betty’s where my mother asked our waiter who was the bartender because depending on who the bartender was depended on what she got. At the end of the diner, my dad handed back the receipt to the waiter and we were off back home after a fantastic day in the city with my family.

paragraphs.body: With receipts in our hands to represent the quality time that we shared as a family to remind us that memories were made. The receipts are always something that just gets thrown anywhere and you will constantly see receipts on the ground while walking around. The receipts always ended up at the bottom of my mother's purse or in my father's pockets at the end of the night. They represent the quality family time that we all share during this busy time of year with everything going on. Even though the receipts will eventually get thrown away in a couple of months when my mother decides to clean out her purse for the first time in months, they will always hold the valuable memories that we made.

annotations.text: They represent the quality family time that we all share during this busy time of year with everything going on. Even though the receipts will eventually get thrown away in a couple of months when my mother decides to clean out her purse for the first time in months, they will always hold the valuable memories that we made.

annotations.text: The receipts always ended up at the bottom of my mother's purse or in my father's pockets at the end of the night.
annotations.comment: Great line! What if you started the paragraph with this line (and then moved right into description)?

paragraphs.body: All of the receipts were always white with black writing on them but what was written varied depending on what was purchased and where it was purchased. The receipt from the vendor with the churros felt warm and crisp because it had just been printed off. It also smelled like cinnamon and sugar like the churros that we had gotten with the receipt. It quickly got placed into my mother's purse and once placed in the purse the sound of paper crumbling happened and the once nice and crisp paper is now crumbled up in between everything and anything.

annotations.text: It also smelled like cinnamon and sugar like the churros that we had gotten with the receipt.
annotations.comment: See! This kind of detail does the work I talked about above :) What other descriptions could you include to do that same kind of work?

paragraphs.body: Family time was always something that my parents cared about but it was always hard to figure out when everyone was free and when we had a free weekend. My parents loved to go out when we all had a free weekend. When Claire and I were not at a softball field or a basketball court or hanging out with our friends. When my older brother, Nick, was not working or on a family vacation with his best friend. Or when my older sister, Elias, was also not working or back from college or hanging out with her friends. The time that we found that all six of us were free and could hang out as a family is always used to its fullest potential. It usually would consist of having a nice family movie night at home where my mom would pick some movie that she thought we all would enjoy and we all would watch it. While my dad is baking some sort of sweet treat that we all will devour in seconds once he says they are done. Other times the family time consists of going out to a restaurant and having a nice family dinner outing most likely at Red Robin, The Trappe, Smokin’ Bettys, or Ralph’s in the city. The dinner outings will usually turn into going somewhere to shop like the Philadelphia Premium Outlets or the King of Prussia Mall to get either Christmas gifts or for back-to-school shopping.

annotations.comment: Again: great details! Just remember to keep that verb tense consistent.
annotations.context_left: thought we all would enjoy and we all would watch it. While my dad is baking some sort of

paragraphs.body: The family outings were usually scheduled in advance because of how busy we all are always and usually were an all-day adventure. The outings always ended with multiple receipts being handed to our parents from either the restaurant or the multiple different shops we went to that day to get whatever we decided that we might need. The family outings sometimes would drag on for too long sometimes and ended up with us four kids starting to bicker with each other more than usual. So our parents would have to figure out a way to distract us and make us stop fighting with each other. That would usually call for my dad to go out and get us all a little snack most likely if we were at the mall or the outlets would be Auntie Anne’s pretzels. So by the end of our adventures, we all would see Auntie Anne’s receipt and remember the time that we started to fight with each other, and our dad would have to go and buy us food to shut us up and make us happy.

paragraphs.body: Family is a group of people who are always there for each other no matter what is going on in their lives. A family is not a group of people who do not support each other. A family is not a singular person who does not care about anyone else. A family is not a group of people who only use each other to get what they want and then throw them away when their purpose is done. A family is not a group of people who do not understand the meaning behind love and caring for others. A family is not a group of people who do not just take each other for granted. A family is a group of people who care and love each other and know what it means to be with each other even if it is just in silence. A family is a group of people who will do anything for each other and sacrifice whatever they have for each other. A family are people who may not be blood-related but are people who they will always trust and respect. A family is a group of people who will always accept you for who you are and nothing else and stand with you.

annotations.text: Family is a group of people who are always there for each other no matter what is going on in their lives.
annotations.comment: Aha! Interesting. How has your family been there for you?
annotations.context_right: A family is not a g

annotations.text: A family is not a group of people who do not just take each other for granted.

paragraphs.body: The receipts were never meant to be the focus of the time we spent together. The receipts were always just the thing to remind us of what we did and what memories were made. The receipts hold the love that we have for each other as a family and will endlessly hold the love that we all share. The memories that we made on these adventures were always the main goal of family time and the new experiences that we all experienced together. The receipts are just the physical things that remind us about what we did. While the memories are always going to be there in our minds forever sometimes we might forget about them until we see the receipt that reminds us of the quality family time we all experienced together.
